jokes.text: No, I think you should deliver the news. / Yeah, but you're the candidate. / Yeah, but you're the brilliant campaign manager. / But you're the cutest. / I love you. / I love you too.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ben get stuck in an endless polite loop about who should deliver news
jokes.punchline: Ben breaks the pattern by calling Leslie 'cutest' leading to mutual 'I love yous'
jokes.explanation: Well-crafted character moment showing their relationship dynamic through awkward politeness spiral

jokes.text: Leslie's fantasy about her swearing-in includes President Obama personally attending
jokes.setup: Leslie describes her victory fantasy
jokes.punchline: Obama not only attends but personally acknowledges her with 'Hey, buddy'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Leslie's delusions of grandeur - Obama casually greeting her as 'buddy'

jokes.text: Luckily, it only hit Jerry, so no damage was done.
jokes.setup: Andy describes a pie attack meant for Leslie
jokes.punchline: Dismisses Jerry getting hit as 'no damage done'
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry abuse running gag delivered with perfect casualness

jokes.text: This is Bert Macklin's greatest assignment yet.
jokes.setup: Andy takes on a simple pie investigation
jokes.punchline: Treats it as his greatest assignment as his alter ego
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Andy's recurring character that shows his commitment to the bit

jokes.text: You can address me as 'eagle one.' Ann, code name: 'Been there, done that.' April is 'currently doing that.' Donna is 'it happened once in a dream.' Chris, code name: 'If I had to pick a dude.' Ben is 'eagle two.'
jokes.setup: Ron assigns security code names
jokes.punchline: The names reveal Ron's sexual history/preferences in increasing detail
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating structure that reveals Ron's sexual history while maintaining his deadpan delivery. Highly quotable and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Ben's relief at being 'Eagle Two' instead of getting a sexual code name
jokes.setup: Andy's pattern of sexual code names for everyone
jokes.punchline: Ben's genuine 'Oh, thank God' relief at getting a normal name
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat - Ben's straight-man horror at Andy's inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: Wha-- I'm on it. I'll go. I'm going. Please, I can go. I don't mind. It's better than listening to her stump speech for a millionth time. No. I will do it. I have to do it. I need to do it. Good-bye.
jokes.setup: Multiple people volunteer for a simple coffee run
jokes.punchline: Chris's increasingly manic insistence on doing this mundane task
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Chris's emotional desperation through mundane task fixation

jokes.text: This has been a difficult year for me, romantically. Millicent Gergich, Ann Perkins, Andy's professor-- lots of disappointment, but if I keep my body moving and my mind occupied at all times, I will avoid falling into a bottomless pit of despair.
jokes.setup: Chris lists his romantic failures
jokes.punchline: His clinical approach to avoiding emotional breakdown
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Chris's struggle with depression through his typical clinical language

jokes.text: And I will finally eliminate this city's libraries!
jokes.setup: Leslie lists her policy goals
jokes.punchline: Accidentally says the opposite of what she means about libraries
jokes.explanation: Good political gaffe moment that plays against Leslie's character

jokes.text: Leslie's stump speech is 10 minutes and 40 seconds every time. Here, check this out. There will be a big laugh right... now.
jokes.setup: Ben times Leslie's perfectly rehearsed speech
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates his ability to predict the audience reaction precisely
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about political campaigns and Ben's analytical nature

jokes.text: And now, a two-second awkward silence after Leslie does her Rodney Dangerfield impression. I tell ya, these geese don't give me no respect. No respect at all. She insists on trying it every time, and it never works.
jokes.setup: Ben predicts Leslie's failed comedy bit
jokes.punchline: Leslie performs the bad impression exactly as predicted
jokes.explanation: Brilliant multi-layered joke that's both cringe comedy and meta-commentary on political speeches

jokes.text: Everybody says they care about the issues, but at the end of the day, all anyone really wants is free clothing shot at them from a cannon.
jokes.setup: Leslie reflects on political campaigning
jokes.punchline: Reduces voter motivation to desire for t-shirt cannons
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about American political culture

jokes.text: Andy recreating the pie trajectory by smashing pie into Jerry's face repeatedly
jokes.setup: Andy investigates the pie incident scientifically
jokes.punchline: Repeatedly hits Jerry with pies 'for accuracy' while Jerry suffers
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy with Jerry as punching bag - escalates nicely

jokes.text: The video shows that the pie came from a high angle, so it would've hit your face... like this. Don't close your eyes.
jokes.setup: Andy recreates the pie trajectory for investigation
jokes.punchline: He's obviously about to hit Jerry with another pie
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy setup that plays on Jerry's victim status

jokes.text: Yeah, it was $900, but now it's $10,000. The Newport campaign offered me 10 grand to just rent the vans and park them somewhere.
jokes.setup: Tom tries to secure the van rental they already paid for
jokes.punchline: The price has jumped to over 10x the original amount
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation showing political dirty tricks through absurd price inflation

jokes.text: Nick Newport has been intimidating and bullying and poisoning this town for years. Frankly, he's a real jerk, and if he thinks he can buy this election for his son, he's wrong. Sorry, I meant can you comment on Nick Newport dying earlier today? He died?
jokes.setup: Leslie gives an angry statement about Nick Newport
jokes.punchline: She learns he died right after calling him a jerk
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe moment with perfect timing and dramatic irony

jokes.text: Sad. Is that your official statement? 'I sad'? No, my official statement is that is overall, a bummer.
jokes.setup: Reporter asks for Leslie's official statement on the death
jokes.punchline: Leslie's increasingly inadequate responses culminating in 'a bummer'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of awkwardness with quotable dialogue and Leslie's characteristic inability to handle crisis

jokes.text: Anything else would be a classless move, on par with spray-painting nipples on the Lincoln memorial.
jokes.setup: Jen calls for Leslie to cancel campaign events
jokes.punchline: Compares continuing to campaign to a bizarre act of vandalism
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific absurd comparison that's both vivid and quotable

jokes.text: Leslie organizing people by how much she trusts them
jokes.setup: Emergency meeting needs organization
jokes.punchline: Leslie literally arranges people in trust order - visual hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie character moment - her need to organize everything, even emotions

jokes.text: Good answer, great body.
jokes.setup: Ben gives political advice during a crisis
jokes.punchline: Leslie inappropriately compliments his body
jokes.explanation: Random Leslie moment that shows her inability to focus during stress

jokes.text: I wasn't listening, but I strongly disagree with Ann.
jokes.setup: April is asked for her opinion
jokes.punchline: Admits she wasn't listening but still disagrees with Ann
jokes.explanation: Perfect April line that captures her character in minimal words

jokes.text: If the--if the guy--if-- Okay.
jokes.setup: Andy tries to give sophisticated political advice
jokes.punchline: He completely gives up mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his limitations with complex topics

jokes.text: What's next on the 'schedge?'
jokes.setup: Leslie asks about the schedule
jokes.punchline: Uses a made-up portmanteau 'schedge'
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie word invention showing her quirky efficiency

jokes.text: I have a business idea for a gourmet alcoholic yogurt. I call it... Yogurt platinum.
jokes.setup: Tom proposes a business deal
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous alcoholic yogurt concept
jokes.explanation: Good Tom business idea that's appropriately ridiculous for his character

jokes.text: 'We're all your friends,' said Mr. bacon. 'Welcome home.' The end. And that is Groffle, the awful Waffle, a book that I wrote and published on behalf of my education initiative.
jokes.setup: Leslie reads from her children's book
jokes.punchline: The terrible story and its awful title 'Groffle, the awful Waffle'
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie character moment showing her well-intentioned but terrible creative efforts

jokes.text: Leslie reading her children's book 'Groffle, the Awful Waffle'
jokes.setup: Leslie promotes her education initiative
jokes.punchline: Her book is called 'Groffle, the Awful Waffle' with Mr. Bacon character
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie overachievement - even her children's book is elaborately branded

jokes.text: I should not have called Nick Newport a jerk, because we need to be respectful of all dead people. I mean, not Stalin or Hitler, but-- I'm not calling Nick Newport Hitler.
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to apologize for calling Newport a jerk
jokes.punchline: Accidentally compares him to Hitler while trying not to
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Leslie digging herself deeper while trying to recover

jokes.text: Leslie's spiral comparing Newport to Hitler then catching herself
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to explain respecting dead people
jokes.punchline: Escalates to 'not Stalin or Hitler' then panics: 'I'm not calling Nick Newport Hitler'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Leslie's panic - trying to clarify makes it worse

jokes.text: Are there any other deceased members of Bobby Newport's family you'd like to attack? And quit ducking the waffle question. Did Groffle use a boat of some kind?
jokes.setup: Reporter asks aggressive political questions
jokes.punchline: Equally insistent about fictional waffle transportation
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment treating children's book logistics like serious journalism

jokes.text: Are we to assume that he swam across the syrup river?
jokes.setup: Reporter continues pursuing the waffle story
jokes.punchline: Treats the fictional logistics as a serious investigative matter
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the absurd waffle journalism bit

jokes.text: I own a fleet of 22 crappy white vans, and for some reason there's a massive bidding war going on over them. Now for a guy whose only assets are 22 crappy white vans, it's pretty much a dream scenario, so I'm just going to ride this out, see where it takes me. And now it's 15,000.
jokes.setup: Bill explains the van pricing situation
jokes.punchline: His honest assessment of his pathetic assets and opportunistic approach
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing realistic opportunistic behavior with self-aware delivery

jokes.text: Leslie Knope is driving her campaign bus through a memorial service. Just when we thought she couldn't stoop any lower.
jokes.setup: Leslie and team arrive at what they thought was a private meeting
jokes.punchline: They've driven a campaign bus through a funeral
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag that maximizes the political disaster with perfect timing

jokes.text: Hi... Sorry, are we late?
jokes.setup: Leslie has just driven through a memorial service
jokes.punchline: Her casual question about being late
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement that caps the funeral disaster sequence

jokes.text: We brought a 'sorry for your loss' fruit basket for Bobby. And it is very classy. No melon, no apples.
jokes.setup: Leslie defends her sincere intentions
jokes.punchline: Her oddly specific criteria for classy fruit
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment showing her misplaced attention to detail

jokes.text: 'Cause I'm smarter and faster. That is not a judgment. That's just a fact.
jokes.setup: Jen explains how she outsmarted Leslie's team
jokes.punchline: Claims it's objective fact rather than opinion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jen's arrogance and competitiveness

jokes.text: Okay, first of all, dark places are awesome. Secondly, Ann is kind of lame, so way to dodge a bullet. And Millicent is Jerry's daughter, so two bullets.
jokes.setup: Chris talks about going to dark emotional places
jokes.punchline: April's brutal assessment of his romantic failures
jokes.explanation: Perfect April response with escalating insults and her characteristic darkness

jokes.text: I'm not fine. It didn't work.
jokes.setup: April gives Chris pep talk about being fine
jokes.punchline: Chris immediately contradicts himself in confessional
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chris's emotional fragility

jokes.text: He was, like, a million years old. And he was kind of a jerk.
jokes.setup: Leslie apologizes for calling Nick Newport a jerk
jokes.punchline: Bobby casually agrees his father was a jerk
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the jerk controversy with Bobby's casual agreement

jokes.text: Your mom and my dad are both dicks.
jokes.setup: Leslie tells heartwarming story about her supportive mother
jokes.punchline: Bobby completely misses the point and insults both parents
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that subverts Leslie's inspirational story with Bobby's obliviousness. Perfect character work and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Or... Leslie wasn't the target. Well, who then? Okay, that's permanent marker. Go find Ben, get a list of everyone he's ever fired. Go, go! Macklin, you son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Andy realizes Leslie wasn't the pie target
jokes.punchline: Dramatic revelation that Ben was the target, plus permanent marker gag
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the investigation plot with Andy's detective breakthrough

jokes.text: And I won. It was awesome. My dad wrapped me in a big hug, said he was proud of me. That's just a great story that happened between me and my dad and nobody else.
jokes.setup: Bobby retells Leslie's inspirational story
jokes.punchline: Changes the ending to make himself the winner and emphasizes it's his story
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Bobby's obliviousness and tendency to rewrite reality in his favor

jokes.text: Bobby's unexpected endorsement of Leslie after she was 'super, super awesome'
jokes.setup: Bobby giving victory speech to supporters
jokes.punchline: Tells them to vote for Leslie instead because she's 'super, super awesome lady'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bobby character moment - childlike honesty trumping political strategy

jokes.text: But you're the opposition campaign manager. I--I couldn't do that to Leslie. Oh, for God's sake, Chris. This campaign is over. My job is done and so is yours. It's just sex. I'm very good at it.
jokes.setup: Jen propositions Chris despite being on opposite campaigns
jokes.punchline: Her blunt dismissal of his concerns and confidence about her skills
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jen's pragmatic approach to both politics and sex

jokes.text: Sewage Joe. Ben Wyatt fired you for sending pictures of your penis to everyone, and you've come here to 'pie' him. Little twerp has it coming.
jokes.setup: Andy solves the pie mystery
jokes.punchline: Reveals the bizarre motive and Sewage Joe's casual acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the mystery with appropriately absurd motive and character name
